channel.title: Getting the Point on Christian Internet Resources
channel.description: Recent content in Getting the Point on Christian Internet Resources

item.title: Hermeneutics: Getting the Point
Interpretation, Hermeneutics, Theological Method
item.description:
INTERPRETATION, HERMENEUTICS, THEOLOGICAL METHOD

Virgil Warren, PhD

In dealing with controversial passages, interpreters make their choice by their understanding of the big picture, the general understanding, the conceptual context. They follow this procedure even with passages that at first glance would call for a more likely-sounding interpretation. The readers may choose a less likely sounding meaning because of the general understanding they bring to the text. In such cases, where more than one reading is possible, the text doesn’t prove either idea that the readers are bringing to it; that text rises no higher than confirmatory evidence in contrast to primary proof. The passage falls out of the process of arriving at the general understanding. That general understanding must come from someplace else or from other places taken together.

item.title: Introduction: The Nature of Language Communication

<p class=MsoNormal align=center style='margin-top:0in;margin-right:-.5in;
margin-bottom:0in;margin-left:.5in;margin-bottom:.0001pt;text-align:center'><b><span
style='font-size:14.0pt'>GETTING THE POINT</span></b></p>

item.title: The Meaning of Interpret
item.description: C. Consequent nature of appropriating scripture (illumination)      Virgil Warren, PhD

item.title: Hindrances to Interpretation: Pride
item.description: 8. Hindrances to interpretation                                                Virgil Warren, PhD

item.title: Hindrances: Presuppositions
item.description: b. Presuppositions (accompanying misinformation)         Virgil Warren, PhD

item.title: Hindrances: Irrationalism
item.description: c. Irrationalism (improper thinking patterns)                    Virgil Warren, PhD

item.title: Streamlining Causal Distinctions
item.description: II.      SELECTED LINGUISTIC PHENOMENA (Note Dr. Warren’s Ph.D. dissertation The Implication of Divine Self-Consistency for the Doctrine of Natural Depravity, pp. 152-70.)

item.title: Interchanging Nature, State, and Action Terms
item.description: B. Interchange of Nature, State, and Action Terms                Virgil Warren, PhD

item.title: The Big Three

item.title: Appendix: Selected Linguistic Phenomena
item.description: II.      SELECTED LINGUISTIC PHENOMENA (Note Dr. Warren’s Ph.D. dissertation The Implication of Divine Self-Consistency for the Doctrine of Natural Depravity, pp. 152-70.)

item.title: Appendix: NT Interpretation of OT Texts
item.description:
NEW TESTAMENT INTERPRETATION

OF OLD TESTAMENT TEXTS

 

Virgil Warren, PhD

item.title: Appendix: Comments on Fulfill
item.description:
COMMENTS ON “FULFILL”

 

Virgil Warren, PhD

 

 

                  We may wonder what definition to put on “fulfill” if it covers such a wide range of ideas as (1) a prediction’s coming to pass, (2) an event that illustrates a generalized principle (Acts 1:20?), (3) the accomplishment of something that was also the purpose of something else (the gospel’s fulfilling the Mosaic law: Matthew 5:17; cp. Romans 3:31?), (4) an obedience that accomplished what was commanded (Romans 13:8-10), (5) a necessary inference from another passage, (6) a meeting of the requirements for something (“This course fulfills the requirement”), and (7) a mere formal similarity (? Matthew 2:18, 23; perhaps 2:15).

item.title: Appendix: Wisdom and Law — Divorce
item.description:
WISDOM AND LAW: DIVORCE

 

Virgil Warren, PhD

 

     

            The difference between law and wisdom as well as the relationship between them showed up in an article on the adult offspring of divorce by Irene Sege published in the Boston Globe (see The Manhattan Mercury for Friday, December 20, 1995, D1). Generalizations about these young people show that their parents’ divorce measurably affects a sizable portion of their children when they grow up.
